'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in [[:en:Nakatsugawa|Nakatsugawa, Gifu]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
The shrine was founded as a branch shrine of '''Suwa Taisha''' in '''Shinano Province''' and enshrines a sacred mirror as its object of worship. The shrine crest is '''Kaji no Ha''' (梶の葉).
== Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
=== Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (中津川市山口)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q132041785]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Suwa Shrine (Nakatsugawa, Yamaguchi) (諏訪神社) |image = {{image requested|cat=Nakatsugawa, Gifu}} |location= [[:en:Nakatsugawa|Nakatsugawa, Gifu]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan |coordinates= {{Coord|35.56306|N|137.54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |established= |main_hall= |festival= |shinto_rituals= |prefecture= {{ill|Nakatsugawa|WD=Q844052}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in [[:en:Nakatsugawa|Nakatsugawa, Gifu]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
The shrine was founded as a branch shrine of '''Suwa Taisha''' in '''Shinano Province''' and enshrines a sacred mirror as its object of worship. The shrine crest is '''Kaji no Ha''' (梶の葉).
== Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
=== Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}
== History == A surviving '''munafuda''' (棟札, ridge plaque) records that the shrine underwent repairs in '''Keichō 16 (1611)''', indicating that it was founded even earlier.
A massive '''cryptomeria tree''', with a circumference of '''8 meters''', is located within the shrine precincts, demonstrating its long history.
According to the '''munafuda''', the current '''honden''' (main hall) was rebuilt in '''Bunka 4 (1801)''' by master carpenter '''Nakamura Denzaemon Zenju''' from '''Miyanokoshi-juku'''. It is made entirely of '''keyaki wood''' and features exquisite carvings of '''eagles, cranes, dragons, koi fish, immortals, and figures from Takasago'''.
In '''Meiji 5 (1872)''', the shrine was designated a '''village shrine''' (村社), and in '''Meiji 41 (1908)''', the nearby '''Sarutobi Shrine''' (founded in Keichō 16) was merged into it.
A further '''merger in Meiji 42 (1909)''' incorporated deities such as '''Izanagi, Izanami, Konohanasakuya-hime, Toyouke-hime, Susanoo, Ōkuninushi, Kagutsuchi, and Sarutahiko'''.
Before World War II, it was designated a '''shinsen-heihaku-ryō shrine''', meaning it received state-sponsored offerings during '''Kinensai''', '''Niinamesai''', and '''Reisai''' ceremonies.
Following the '''Shinto Directive''' of '''Shōwa 21 (1946)''', the shrine transitioned into a '''religious corporation'''.
A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

The shrine was founded as a branch shrine of '''Suwa Taisha''' in '''Shinano Province''' and enshrines a sacred mirror as its object of worship. The shrine crest is '''Kaji no Ha''' (梶の葉).
== Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
=== Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}
== History == A surviving '''munafuda''' (棟札, ridge plaque) records that the shrine underwent repairs in '''Keichō 16 (1611)''', indicating that it was founded even earlier.
A massive '''cryptomeria tree''', with a circumference of '''8 meters''', is located within the shrine precincts, demonstrating its long history.
According to the '''munafuda''', the current '''honden''' (main hall) was rebuilt in '''Bunka 4 (1801)''' by master carpenter '''Nakamura Denzaemon Zenju''' from '''Miyanokoshi-juku'''. It is made entirely of '''keyaki wood''' and features exquisite carvings of '''eagles, cranes, dragons, koi fish, immortals, and figures from Takasago'''.
In '''Meiji 5 (1872)''', the shrine was designated a '''village shrine''' (村社), and in '''Meiji 41 (1908)''', the nearby '''Sarutobi Shrine''' (founded in Keichō 16) was merged into it.
A further '''merger in Meiji 42 (1909)''' incorporated deities such as '''Izanagi, Izanami, Konohanasakuya-hime, Toyouke-hime, Susanoo, Ōkuninushi, Kagutsuchi, and Sarutahiko'''.
Before World War II, it was designated a '''shinsen-heihaku-ryō shrine''', meaning it received state-sponsored offerings during '''Kinensai''', '''Niinamesai''', and '''Reisai''' ceremonies.
Following the '''Shinto Directive''' of '''Shōwa 21 (1946)''', the shrine transitioned into a '''religious corporation'''.
A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (中津川市山口)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (中津川市山口)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (中津川市山口)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q132041785]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Suwa Shrine (Nakatsugawa, Yamaguchi) (諏訪神社) |image = {{image requested|cat=Nakatsugawa, Gifu}} |location= [[:en:Nakatsugawa|Nakatsugawa, Gifu]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan |coordinates= {{Coord|35.56306|N|137.54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |established= |main_hall= |festival= |shinto_rituals= |prefecture= {{ill|Nakatsugawa|WD=Q844052}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in [[:en:Nakatsugawa|Nakatsugawa, Gifu]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
The shrine was founded as a branch shrine of '''Suwa Taisha''' in '''Shinano Province''' and enshrines a sacred mirror as its object of worship. The shrine crest is '''Kaji no Ha''' (梶の葉).
== Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
=== Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}
== History == A surviving '''munafuda''' (棟札, ridge plaque) records that the shrine underwent repairs in '''Keichō 16 (1611)''', indicating that it was founded even earlier.
A massive '''cryptomeria tree''', with a circumference of '''8 meters''', is located within the shrine precincts, demonstrating its long history.
According to the '''munafuda''', the current '''honden''' (main hall) was rebuilt in '''Bunka 4 (1801)''' by master carpenter '''Nakamura Denzaemon Zenju''' from '''Miyanokoshi-juku'''. It is made entirely of '''keyaki wood''' and features exquisite carvings of '''eagles, cranes, dragons, koi fish, immortals, and figures from Takasago'''.
In '''Meiji 5 (1872)''', the shrine was designated a '''village shrine''' (村社), and in '''Meiji 41 (1908)''', the nearby '''Sarutobi Shrine''' (founded in Keichō 16) was merged into it.
A further '''merger in Meiji 42 (1909)''' incorporated deities such as '''Izanagi, Izanami, Konohanasakuya-hime, Toyouke-hime, Susanoo, Ōkuninushi, Kagutsuchi, and Sarutahiko'''.
Before World War II, it was designated a '''shinsen-heihaku-ryō shrine''', meaning it received state-sponsored offerings during '''Kinensai''', '''Niinamesai''', and '''Reisai''' ceremonies.
Following the '''Shinto Directive''' of '''Shōwa 21 (1946)''', the shrine transitioned into a '''religious corporation'''.
A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (中津川市山口)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (中津川市山口)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (中津川市山口)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q132041785]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Suwa Shrine (Nakatsugawa, Yamaguchi) (諏訪神社) |image = {{image requested|cat=Nakatsugawa, Gifu}} |location= [[:en:Nakatsugawa|Nakatsugawa, Gifu]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan |coordinates= {{Coord|35.56306|N|137.54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |established= |main_hall= |festival= |shinto_rituals= |prefecture= {{ill|Nakatsugawa|WD=Q844052}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in [[:en:Nakatsugawa|Nakatsugawa, Gifu]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
The shrine was founded as a branch shrine of '''Suwa Taisha''' in '''Shinano Province''' and enshrines a sacred mirror as its object of worship. The shrine crest is '''Kaji no Ha''' (梶の葉).
== Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
=== Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}
== History == A surviving '''munafuda''' (棟札, ridge plaque) records that the shrine underwent repairs in '''Keichō 16 (1611)''', indicating that it was founded even earlier.
A massive '''cryptomeria tree''', with a circumference of '''8 meters''', is located within the shrine precincts, demonstrating its long history.
According to the '''munafuda''', the current '''honden''' (main hall) was rebuilt in '''Bunka 4 (1801)''' by master carpenter '''Nakamura Denzaemon Zenju''' from '''Miyanokoshi-juku'''. It is made entirely of '''keyaki wood''' and features exquisite carvings of '''eagles, cranes, dragons, koi fish, immortals, and figures from Takasago'''.
In '''Meiji 5 (1872)''', the shrine was designated a '''village shrine''' (村社), and in '''Meiji 41 (1908)''', the nearby '''Sarutobi Shrine''' (founded in Keichō 16) was merged into it.
A further '''merger in Meiji 42 (1909)''' incorporated deities such as '''Izanagi, Izanami, Konohanasakuya-hime, Toyouke-hime, Susanoo, Ōkuninushi, Kagutsuchi, and Sarutahiko'''.
Before World War II, it was designated a '''shinsen-heihaku-ryō shrine''', meaning it received state-sponsored offerings during '''Kinensai''', '''Niinamesai''', and '''Reisai''' ceremonies.
Following the '''Shinto Directive''' of '''Shōwa 21 (1946)''', the shrine transitioned into a '''religious corporation'''.
A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in [[:en:Nakatsugawa|Nakatsugawa, Gifu]], Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
The shrine was founded as a branch shrine of '''Suwa Taisha''' in '''Shinano Province''' and enshrines a sacred mirror as its object of worship. The shrine crest is '''Kaji no Ha''' (梶の葉).
== Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
=== Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}
== History == A surviving '''munafuda''' (棟札, ridge plaque) records that the shrine underwent repairs in '''Keichō 16 (1611)''', indicating that it was founded even earlier.
A massive '''cryptomeria tree''', with a circumference of '''8 meters''', is located within the shrine precincts, demonstrating its long history.
According to the '''munafuda''', the current '''honden''' (main hall) was rebuilt in '''Bunka 4 (1801)''' by master carpenter '''Nakamura Denzaemon Zenju''' from '''Miyanokoshi-juku'''. It is made entirely of '''keyaki wood''' and features exquisite carvings of '''eagles, cranes, dragons, koi fish, immortals, and figures from Takasago'''.
In '''Meiji 5 (1872)''', the shrine was designated a '''village shrine''' (村社), and in '''Meiji 41 (1908)''', the nearby '''Sarutobi Shrine''' (founded in Keichō 16) was merged into it.
A further '''merger in Meiji 42 (1909)''' incorporated deities such as '''Izanagi, Izanami, Konohanasakuya-hime, Toyouke-hime, Susanoo, Ōkuninushi, Kagutsuchi, and Sarutahiko'''.
Before World War II, it was designated a '''shinsen-heihaku-ryō shrine''', meaning it received state-sponsored offerings during '''Kinensai''', '''Niinamesai''', and '''Reisai''' ceremonies.
Following the '''Shinto Directive''' of '''Shōwa 21 (1946)''', the shrine transitioned into a '''religious corporation'''.
A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in {{ill|Nakatsugawa|en|Nakatsugawa|WD=Q844052|lt=Nakatsugawa, Gifu}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
The shrine was founded as a branch shrine of '''Suwa Taisha''' in '''Shinano Province''' and enshrines a sacred mirror as its object of worship. The shrine crest is '''Kaji no Ha''' (梶の葉).
== Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}
=== Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}
== History == A surviving '''munafuda''' (棟札, ridge plaque) records that the shrine underwent repairs in '''Keichō 16 (1611)''', indicating that it was founded even earlier.
A massive '''cryptomeria tree''', with a circumference of '''8 meters''', is located within the shrine precincts, demonstrating its long history.
According to the '''munafuda''', the current '''honden''' (main hall) was rebuilt in '''Bunka 4 (1801)''' by master carpenter '''Nakamura Denzaemon Zenju''' from '''Miyanokoshi-juku'''. It is made entirely of '''keyaki wood''' and features exquisite carvings of '''eagles, cranes, dragons, koi fish, immortals, and figures from Takasago'''.
In '''Meiji 5 (1872)''', the shrine was designated a '''village shrine''' (村社), and in '''Meiji 41 (1908)''', the nearby '''Sarutobi Shrine''' (founded in Keichō 16) was merged into it.
A further '''merger in Meiji 42 (1909)''' incorporated deities such as '''Izanagi, Izanami, Konohanasakuya-hime, Toyouke-hime, Susanoo, Ōkuninushi, Kagutsuchi, and Sarutahiko'''.
Before World War II, it was designated a '''shinsen-heihaku-ryō shrine''', meaning it received state-sponsored offerings during '''Kinensai''', '''Niinamesai''', and '''Reisai''' ceremonies.
Following the '''Shinto Directive''' of '''Shōwa 21 (1946)''', the shrine transitioned into a '''religious corporation'''.
A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Suwa Shrine (Nakatsugawa, Yamaguchi) (諏訪神社) |image = {{image requested|cat=Nakatsugawa, Gifu}} |location= {{ill|Nakatsugawa|en|Nakatsugawa|WD=Q844052|lt=Nakatsugawa, Gifu}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan |coordinates= {{Coord|35.56306|N|137.54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |established= |main_hall= |festival= |shinto_rituals= |prefecture= {{ill|Nakatsugawa|WD=Q844052}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in {{ill|Nakatsugawa|en|Nakatsugawa|WD=Q844052|lt=Nakatsugawa, Gifu}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
The shrine was founded as a branch shrine of '''Suwa Taisha''' in '''Shinano Province''' and enshrines a sacred mirror as its object of worship. The shrine crest is '''Kaji no Ha''' (梶の葉).
== Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}
=== Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}
== History == A surviving '''munafuda''' (棟札, ridge plaque) records that the shrine underwent repairs in '''Keichō 16 (1611)''', indicating that it was founded even earlier.
A massive '''cryptomeria tree''', with a circumference of '''8 meters''', is located within the shrine precincts, demonstrating its long history.
According to the '''munafuda''', the current '''honden''' (main hall) was rebuilt in '''Bunka 4 (1801)''' by master carpenter '''Nakamura Denzaemon Zenju''' from '''Miyanokoshi-juku'''. It is made entirely of '''keyaki wood''' and features exquisite carvings of '''eagles, cranes, dragons, koi fish, immortals, and figures from Takasago'''.
In '''Meiji 5 (1872)''', the shrine was designated a '''village shrine''' (村社), and in '''Meiji 41 (1908)''', the nearby '''Sarutobi Shrine''' (founded in Keichō 16) was merged into it.
A further '''merger in Meiji 42 (1909)''' incorporated deities such as '''Izanagi, Izanami, Konohanasakuya-hime, Toyouke-hime, Susanoo, Ōkuninushi, Kagutsuchi, and Sarutahiko'''.
Before World War II, it was designated a '''shinsen-heihaku-ryō shrine''', meaning it received state-sponsored offerings during '''Kinensai''', '''Niinamesai''', and '''Reisai''' ceremonies.
Following the '''Shinto Directive''' of '''Shōwa 21 (1946)''', the shrine transitioned into a '''religious corporation'''.
A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in {{ill|Nakatsugawa|en|Nakatsugawa|WD=Q844052|lt=Nakatsugawa, Gifu|1=Nakatsugawa}}, Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
The shrine was founded as a branch shrine of '''Suwa Taisha''' in '''Shinano Province''' and enshrines a sacred mirror as its object of worship. The shrine crest is '''Kaji no Ha''' (梶の葉).
== Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}
=== Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}
== History == A surviving '''munafuda''' (棟札, ridge plaque) records that the shrine underwent repairs in '''Keichō 16 (1611)''', indicating that it was founded even earlier.
A massive '''cryptomeria tree''', with a circumference of '''8 meters''', is located within the shrine precincts, demonstrating its long history.
According to the '''munafuda''', the current '''honden''' (main hall) was rebuilt in '''Bunka 4 (1801)''' by master carpenter '''Nakamura Denzaemon Zenju''' from '''Miyanokoshi-juku'''. It is made entirely of '''keyaki wood''' and features exquisite carvings of '''eagles, cranes, dragons, koi fish, immortals, and figures from Takasago'''.
In '''Meiji 5 (1872)''', the shrine was designated a '''village shrine''' (村社), and in '''Meiji 41 (1908)''', the nearby '''Sarutobi Shrine''' (founded in Keichō 16) was merged into it.
A further '''merger in Meiji 42 (1909)''' incorporated deities such as '''Izanagi, Izanami, Konohanasakuya-hime, Toyouke-hime, Susanoo, Ōkuninushi, Kagutsuchi, and Sarutahiko'''.
Before World War II, it was designated a '''shinsen-heihaku-ryō shrine''', meaning it received state-sponsored offerings during '''Kinensai''', '''Niinamesai''', and '''Reisai''' ceremonies.
Following the '''Shinto Directive''' of '''Shōwa 21 (1946)''', the shrine transitioned into a '''religious corporation'''.
A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]

A '''400-year-old cryptomeria tree''', which once stood '''53 meters tall''' and had a '''circumference of 3.8 meters''', was cut down in '''Shōwa 40 (1965)''' to expand '''Route 19'''.
The area was originally part of '''Yamaguchi Village, Kiso District, Nagano Prefecture''', but due to a '''cross-prefectural merger on February 13, 2005''', it is now part of '''Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture'''.
On '''June 3, 1993 (Heisei 5)''', the '''Yamaguchi Village Board of Education''' designated the '''honden''' as a '''tangible cultural property''', which is now registered under '''Nakatsugawa City'''.
== Festivals == The annual festival is held on '''July 26 and 27''', with the '''main festival (Honmatsuri) on July 27''' featuring the '''Hanamatsuri (Flower Horse Festival)'''.
From '''July 20''', two villages within the former '''Yamaguchi Village''' take turns crafting '''flower decorations''' for the horses.
Records indicate that in '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Hanamatsuri''' was already being held at '''Nangū Shrine in Tadai Village''', suggesting that the practice was introduced to the area at that time.
The '''flower decorations''' mimic '''chrysanthemums''' and '''susuki grass''', with each household receiving one for '''pest control in their fields'''.
During the two-day festival, '''4th and 5th grade girls''' perform a '''sacred dance''' that has been passed down since the '''Edo period'''.
Before '''World War II''', young men undergoing their '''military draft examination''' were responsible for '''organizing the festival'''.
On '''July 26''', the '''portable shrine (mikoshi)''' is paraded around the shrine precinct five times, and on '''July 27''', it is carried seven times.
== Insect Exorcism (Mushi Okuri) == Until '''Shōwa 32 (1957)''', an '''insect-exorcism ritual''' was performed on '''July 28''', the day after the festival.
Farmers would capture '''pests such as stink bugs, fleas, flies, and rice weevils''', wrap them in '''paper along with rice grains''', and tie them to '''bamboo branches''' adorned with '''colored paper strips'''.
The '''bamboo was then carried to Suwa Shrine''' for purification before being thrown into the '''Kiso River from Amagoi Bridge'''.
The practice was abandoned due to the '''construction of Yamaguchi Dam''', which altered the river's flow and caused concerns about '''pollution'''.
== Shrine Precincts == The shrine grounds include a '''kaguraden''', '''worship hall''', and '''Awa Shrine'''.
== Sacred Grove (Shasō) == The shrine’s forest features '''warm-temperate species like Aritaki and Sakaki''', despite its '''inland, cold-climate location'''.
A '''rare "Shidare-Enoki" (weeping hackberry tree)''' is also present.
In '''Taishō 14 (1925)''', a teacher from '''Matsumoto Women's Normal School''' declared it a '''one-of-a-kind specimen worthy of national protection''', submitting a petition to the '''Ministry of Home Affairs'''.
Originally designated a '''Natural Monument of Nagano Prefecture''', the shrine forest was '''transferred to Gifu Prefecture''' after the '''2005 merger''', and was officially designated as a '''Gifu Prefectural Natural Monument on September 5, 2006'''.
== References == * '''Yamaguchi Village History, Volume 2''' (1995) * '''Shinano Temples & Shrines''' (Shinano Mainichi Shimbun, 1981)
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7411.html Gifu Prefecture Official Site] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=3352 Gifu Shrine Directory]
